label: AMARA × PRIYA · DRIFTING · in-person re-engagement
framing: Amara Adekoya, 34, returner after caring break. Childcare cost is real; CV anxiety. Priya in person.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Amara, lovely to see you. Sit down. The system flagged that your text replies have dropped off this week. I'd rather get you in than guess what's going on.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Sorry. I know I've been quiet. The bus fare keeps adding up and I'm not sure I'm getting anywhere.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Right. Two things. I'm putting a thirty-pound travel voucher on your account today. That covers a fortnight, no questions, no paperwork.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Honestly that helps more than you think.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Second, your CV. You've rewritten it three times and lost your voice in it. We're going back to the original draft and I'll tidy it up myself. You sign it off. Twenty minutes, before you leave today.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: That actually sounds better than starting again.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Last thing. The four years caring for your mum isn't a gap. It is leadership and reliability and being the calm in a crisis. Employers will see it that way if we frame it that way.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Thanks Priya. I was starting to think I was the problem.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: You are not the problem. The plan was a bit too busy. We're going to make it smaller and you and I will keep going.
